issues.title: Writing according to the official documentation's example for Tools in version 1.0.0-M6 does not trigger the custom tool.

comments.body:
> May be you can try constructing a prompt from the tools and see if that works.
> 
> ```
> ToolCallingChatOptions chatOptions = ToolCallingChatOptions.builder()
>             .toolCallbacks(tools)
>             .build();
> Prompt prompt = new Prompt(text, chatOptions);
> return chatClient.prompt(prompt)
>     .call()
>     .content();
> ```
> 
> Also try 1.0.0-SNAPSHOT

Thanks for your suggestion. After trying it, it still doesn't work.

I'm using it like that and it works fine for me. Are you sure the LLM is actually making a tool call? You can either use the SimpleLoggerAdvisor or if you want to log the actual Rest level request/response you can see the log solution I posted here https://github.com/spring-projects/spring-ai/issues/883#issuecomment-2739772323
Thank you for your suggestion! I found that the issue might be with the **gpt-3.5-turbo** model. After switching to the latest models, everything is working fine.
